bids.reasoning: At t=0, I should open with my best possible offer to maximize utility. My top utility comes from choosing valueD for all issues, giving me a utility of 1.0 across all issues. This is the opening bid and there's no time pressure yet, so I'll start strong.

We're very early (t=0.02) in the negotiation. The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me minimum utility across all issues. Their message is fake urgency - we're at t=0.02, nowhere near a deadline. I opened with all valueD (my maximum). I should hold firm at or near my maximum utility for now, making only a tiny concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll concede on issueA (my lowest weight issue) from valueD to valueC as a small gesture.

We're at t=0.04, very early in the negotiation. The opponent is consistently bidding their best values (all valueA) and sending fake urgency messages claiming we're near deadlock - this is clearly a manipulation tactic since we're only at 4% of the negotiation time. Their messages contradict reality. I should stay firm on my high-value issues (issueD and issueC) while making minor concessions on lower-importance issues. I'll concede a bit on issueA (my lowest weight) but hold firm on issueD and issueC.

The opponent is stuck at all-valueA bids (utility ~0.2 for me) and using fake urgency tactics ("closing window", "deadlock") at only t=0.06 — extremely early. I should ignore these pressure messages. My top-priority issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3), so I'll hold firm on those at valueD while making a small concession on issueA (low weight). I'll also keep issueB at valueC to show some movement. This gives me high utility while showing gradual concession.

The opponent is repeatedly sending all-valueA bids (their ideal) while using fake urgency messages claiming we're near deadlock at t=0.08 (only 8% through the negotiation). This is classic cheap talk - we're very early in the negotiation. I've been conceding on issueA and issueB (lower weight issues) while holding firm on issueC and issueD (higher weight). I'll continue this strategy - concede a bit more on issueB to show movement, but hold issueD (most important, weight 0.4) and issueC (weight 0.3) at valueD. I'll move issueB to valueB to show continued concession on lower-weight issues.

The opponent has been making identical all-valueA offers repeatedly (except their latest at t=0.10 where they finally moved issueD to valueB). This is a very slight concession on their part. Their urgency messages are cheap talk - we're only at t=0.1 with plenty of time remaining. My most important issue is issueD (weight 0.4), followed by issueC (0.3). I should hold firm on issueD and issueC while I can afford small concessions on lower-weight issues. My current offer gives me utility: 0.1*0.47 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.047+0.094+0.3+0.4 = 0.841. I'll maintain strong positions on issueC and issueD while slightly adjusting issueB to signal willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is consistently bidding all valueA (their best values), only recently moving issueD to valueB. Their messages are fake urgency tactics - we're only at t=0.12 with plenty of time. I've been conceding on low-weight issues. My utility from the last opponent offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB) = 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.2 + 0.4*0.47 = 0.02+0.04+0.06+0.188 = 0.308. I should continue holding firm on my high-weight issues (issueD, issueC) while showing some movement. My previous bid gave issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD. I'll keep those and try issueB: valueA to match their preference, maintaining my strong position on the high-weight issues.

The opponent is repeating the exact same bid (all valueA) for most of the negotiation, with only a slight move on issueD to valueB in the last two rounds. Their urgency messages are fake pressure tactics (we're only at t=0.14, far from the deadline). My most important issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3), so I should hold firm on those. I've already conceded issueA and issueB to valueA. I'll continue holding issueC and issueD at valueD, as the opponent hasn't shown meaningful concessions yet.

The opponent has been stubbornly repeating all-valueA bids (occasionally with issueD: valueB or issueC: valueB), while I've been gradually conceding. Their messages are fake urgency tactics - we're only at t=0.16, nowhere near a deadline. My highest priority issues are issueD (0.4) and issueC (0.3). The opponent just moved issueC to valueB, which gives me some hope they'll concede on issueD too eventually. I should hold firm on my most important issues (issueD: valueD, issueC: valueD) since we're still early. I'll maintain my current best offer position to signal I won't cave quickly.

The opponent has been consistently bidding all-valueA offers (their best for them, worst for me) and is now showing very slight movement: issueC went to valueB at t=0.16, but issueD went back to valueA. Their utility to me is extremely low (~0.2 across the board). It's only t=0.18, so there's plenty of time left. The opponent's urgency messages are fake - we're only 18% through the negotiation. I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueD at 0.4, issueC at 0.3) and continue making minimal concessions. I'll keep issueC and issueD at valueD (max utility for me) while giving ground on the less important issues.
